steps.title: Identify the three points from the coordinate grid
Find the center of the circle
steps.content:
The center lies on the perpendicular bisector of every chord. The chord from $(7, 11)$ to $(7, 5)$ is vertical with midpoint $(7, 8)$, so its perpendicular bisector is the horizontal line $y = 8$. Thus the center has $y$-coordinate $8$. Checking the candidate $(7, 8)$ against all three given points:
  • $(7, 8)$ to $(4, 8)$: $|7-4| = 3$
  • $(7, 8)$ to $(7, 11)$: $|11-8| = 3$
  • $(7, 8)$ to $(7, 5)$: $|8-5| = 3$
All three distances are equal, so $(7, 8)$ is the center.

steps.title: Calculate the circumference and find $k$
steps.content:
With radius $r = 3$:
$$C = 2\pi r = 2\pi(3) = 6\pi$$Therefore $k = 6$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
